text:
{"steps": [{"step": 1, "frames": [{"frame_type": "concept", "text": "Light can knock electrons out of metals! This is the **photoelectric effect**.", "highlight": ["photoelectric effect"], "speech": "Have you ever wondered how light can actually make things happen, like pushing tiny particles out of a metal? Well, it's a super cool process called the photoelectric effect!", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000}, {"frame_type": "concept", "text": "Light isn't just a wave, it's also made of tiny energy packets called **photons**.", "highlight": ["photons"], "speech": "Think of light not just as waves, but as tiny packets of energy. We call these packets photons!", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3500}, {"frame_type": "diagram", "text": "Photon hits metal -> Electron ejected", "highlight": ["Photon", "Electron"], "speech": "Imagine a photon hitting a metal surface. If it has enough energy, it can actually give that energy to an electron and kick it right out!", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4500, "svg_html": "<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,user-scalable=no"><style>*{margin:0;padding:0}body{background:#1A2B1A;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:100%;height:100vh}}svg{max-width:100%}</style></head><body><svg viewBox="0 0 400 300" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="100.0" y="100.0" width="200.0" height="100.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" fill="none" fill-opacity="0" rx="4" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="0.00s" fill="freeze"/></rect>
<circle cx="150.0" cy="150.0" r="10.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" fill="none" fill-opacity="0" stroke-dasharray="62.83" stroke-dashoffset="62.83"><animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="62.83" to="0" dur="0.55s" begin="0.30s" fill="freeze"/></circle>
<circle cx="350.0" cy="150.0" r="10.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" fill="none" fill-opacity="0" stroke-dasharray="62.83" stroke-dashoffset="62.83"><animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="62.83" to="0" dur="0.55s" begin="0.78s" fill="freeze"/></circle>
<line x1="200.0" y1="150.0" x2="300.0" y2="150.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-dasharray="100.00" stroke-dashoffset="100.00"><animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="100.00" to="0" dur="0.45s" begin="1.26s" fill="freeze"/></line>
<text x="160.0" y="80.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">Metal Surface<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="1.64s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="130.0" y="160.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">Electron<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="1.92s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="330.0" y="160.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">Photon<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="2.20s" fill="freeze"/></text></svg></body></html>"}], "image_show_confidencescore": 0.0}, {"step": 2, "frames": [{"frame_type": "concept", "text": "Each photon carries a specific amount of **energy**.", "highlight": ["energy"], "speech": "Now, each photon has its own special amount of energy. It's like each little packet is carrying a certain amount of 'oomph'!", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3000}, {"frame_type": "concept", "text": "Electrons are held in the metal by **binding energy**.", "highlight": ["binding energy"], "speech": "But the electrons aren't just floating around freely. They're held tightly within the metal by something called binding energy.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3500}, {"frame_type": "quiz_mcq", "text": "What must a photon's energy be greater than to eject an electron?", "highlight": ["photon's energy", "eject an electron"], "speech": "To get an electron out, the photon's energy needs to overcome a certain barrier. What do we call that barrier?", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "quiz", "duration_ms": 4000, "quiz_options": ["The metal's color", "The binding energy", "The electron's speed", "The photon's wavelength"], "quiz_correct_index": 1}]}, {"step": 3, "frames": [{"frame_type": "concept", "text": "If Photon Energy > Binding Energy, then **energy transfer** occurs.", "highlight": ["energy transfer"], "speech": "So, here's the key: if the energy carried by the photon is MORE than the binding energy holding the electron, then we have a successful energy transfer!", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000}, {"frame_type": "diagram", "text": "Photon Energy (E_p) vs. Binding Energy (E_b)", "highlight": ["E_p", "E_b"], "speech": "We can represent this with a simple idea: if the photon's energy, E_p, is greater than the binding energy, E_b, the electron is ejected.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000, "svg_html": "<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,user-scalable=no"><style>*{margin:0;padding:0}body{background:#1A2B1A;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:100%;height:100vh}}svg{max-width:100%}</style></head><body><svg viewBox="0 0 400 300" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="50.0" y="100.0" width="150.0" height="50.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" fill="none" fill-opacity="0" rx="4" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="0.00s" fill="freeze"/></rect>
<rect x="200.0" y="100.0" width="150.0" height="50.0" stroke="#F0EDD0" stroke-width="2.5" fill="none" fill-opacity="0" rx="4" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="0.30s" fill="freeze"/></rect>
<text x="100.0" y="125.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">E_p<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="0.60s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="250.0" y="125.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">E_b<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="0.88s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="150.0" y="160.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">><animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="1.16s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="100.0" y="70.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">Photon Energy<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="1.44s" fill="freeze"/></text>
<text x="250.0" y="70.0" font-family="serif" font-size="13" fill="#F5E3A0" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0">Binding Energy<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="1" dur="0.35s" begin="1.72s" fill="freeze"/></text></svg></body></html>"}, {"frame_type": "memory", "text": "Photon's energy is the 'push', binding energy is the 'grip'. Push > Grip = Electron Flip!", "highlight": ["Push", "Grip", "Push > Grip = Electron Flip!"], "speech": "Here's a fun way to remember it: Think of the photon's energy as a 'push' and the binding energy as the 'grip'. If the push is greater than the grip, the electron flips right out!", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000}], "image_show_confidencescore": 0.0}, {"step": 4, "frames": [{"frame_type": "quiz_typed", "text": "What is the term for light energy packets?", "highlight": ["light energy packets"], "speech": "Let's test your memory! What do we call those tiny packets of light energy?", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "quiz", "duration_ms": 3000, "quiz_model_answer": "Photons", "quiz_keywords": ["photons", "light packets", "energy packets", "quanta"]}, {"frame_type": "quiz_order", "text": "Order the steps for the photoelectric effect:", "highlight": ["photoelectric effect"], "speech": "Can you put these steps of the photoelectric effect in the correct order?", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "quiz", "duration_ms": 5000, "quiz_options": ["Electron is ejected", "Photon strikes metal", "Photon energy is greater than binding energy"], "quiz_correct_order": [1, 2, 0]}, {"frame_type": "summary", "text": "**Recap:** Light packets (**photons**) transfer **energy** to electrons. If photon energy exceeds **binding energy**, **energy transfer** ejects the electron (**photoelectric effect**).", "highlight": ["photons", "energy", "binding energy", "energy transfer", "photoelectric effect"], "speech": "To sum it all up: The photoelectric effect happens when light, acting as tiny energy packets called photons, transfers enough energy to electrons in a metal. If this energy is greater than the electron's binding energy, the electron gets ejected. Pretty neat, right?", "tts_engine": "google", "voice_role": "assistant", "duration_ms": 5000}]}]}
